name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
"A military band played ‘Hail to the Chief,’ cannons sang out a 21-gun salute and the presidential jet took to the sky..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8f67414393&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **_REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS_**
---
| | | [‘A Great and Noble Man’: Bush Honored One Last Time in Washington](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fe97e430a6&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Washington Times_
“A military band played ‘Hail to the Chief,’ cannons sang out a 21-gun salute and the presidential jet took to the sky, lifting former President George H.W. Bush away from Washington one last time,” Stephen Dinan writes. “The capital gave a solemn but fervent farewell Wednesday to the 41st president, whose death [last] Friday at 94 launched a week of commemorations.”

**In photos:**[America’s solemn farewell to its 41st President](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=abacaae229&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [Trump Declares Day of Mourning for Bush](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=de0d9724ee&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Wall Street Journal_
“President Trump declared Wednesday a national day of mourning for former President George Herbert Walker Bush,” Courtney McBride and Michael Wright report. “Along with his full life of service to country, we will remember President Bush for his devotion to family—especially the love of his life, Barbara,” President Trump said in a statement with First Lady Melania Trump.

**Watch:**[President Trump and the First Lady pay their respects ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8dfe94a396&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| [Trumps Meet Privately with George W., Laura Bush to Offer Condolences](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9605282e56&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Hill_
“President Trump and first lady Melania Trump met privately Tuesday evening with former President George W. Bush and former first lady Laura Bush on the eve of funeral services for former President George H.W. Bush,” Brett Samuels reports. Thousands of Americans visited the U.S. Capitol this week to pay their respects.
---
|
---
| [Vice President Pence: George H.W. Bush ‘Never Failed to Answer the Call to Serve His Country’](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a2c49e1f7c&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Washington Free Beacon_
As former President Bush lay in state at the U.S. Capitol Monday night, Vice President Mike Pence honored the 41st President’s extraordinary life. “While he was known as the ‘quiet man,’ it was not for lack of nerve or daring,” the Vice President said as President George W. Bush and other family members looked on.
---
|
---
| [Trump Celebrates Hanukkah at the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4acd3ab79b&e=3ee4985eea)
-CBS News
President Trump and First Lady Melania Trump hosted two Hanukkah receptions in the White House East Room on Thursday, celebrating the fifth night of the Festival of Lights. In his remarks, the President condemned anti-Semitism and reflected on the horrific shooting at the Tree of Life synagogue this year. Thursday’s receptions came exactly one year after President Trump announced that the U.S. Embassy in Israel would be moved to Jerusalem.
---
|
---
| [Laura Bush Thanks Melania Trump for a ‘Sweet Visit’ to the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=604bf0c522&e=3ee4985eea)
-_The Hill_
Emily Birnbaum reports that First Lady Melania Trump hosted members of the Bush family, including former First Lady Laura Bush, for a special tour of the White House Christmas decorations this week. “A sweet visit during this somber week," the former First Lady wrote on Instagram. “Thanks to Mrs. Trump for inviting our family.”
---
|
---
| [Liz Peek: Trump Scores Big Wins at the G-20](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2262d94e21&e=3ee4985eea)
-Fox News
“President Trump scored major successes at the G-20 summit that concluded over the weekend in Argentina. Specifically, the community of nations agreed in their official communique to ‘necessary reform’ of the World Trade Organization, a top White House priority,” financial expert Liz Peek writes. “The Chinese promised to up their purchases of U.S.-made goods and to discuss other demands in exchange for postponing an expected hike in tariffs.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=be539f4c2d&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f1ee07af22&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=df9179b66e)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

name: Aisling Kerins
subject: We have SO much to do
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Don't let all our hard work go to waste.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ac19/2f5ea88d/7a8578c7/2cbda0e2/934609615/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-7_freeze-2&refcode=ema_2018-12-7_freeze-2&utm_campaign=freeze-2&utm_medium=ema") Friend, We have so much to accomplish in the upcoming months. Jay's going to tackle the work in front of us the same way he tackled 2018: Full steam ahead. Now that our state legislature in Washington is unequivocally progressive thanks to the last election, there's so much we can accomplish on behalf of working people. But it also means right-wing special interests will stop at nothing to bring us down. Climate change deniers, the NRA, and the special interests who want to keep the tax code as it is will come after us with everything they got. [**We have to show these naysayers that we are strong enough to stand up to them -- so we're setting a huge grassroots goal of getting 2,000 donations before our legally mandated fundraising freeze starts on Friday. Will you help us out?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ac19/2f5ea88d/7a8578c7/2cbda0e2/934609615/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-7_freeze-2&refcode=ema_2018-12-7_freeze-2&utm_campaign=freeze-2&utm_medium=ema") [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ac19/2f5ea88d/7a8578c7/2cbda0e2/934609615/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-7_freeze-2&refcode=ema_2018-12-7_freeze-2&utm_campaign=freeze-2&utm_medium=ema") This legally mandated fundraising freeze during our state legislative session makes the work ahead tough. We can't raise a dime for the reelection account while the conservative forces opposing Jay's agenda can raise limitlessly from special interests, lobbyists, and the powerful. **That means this fundraising freeze deadline is a make-or-break moment for our movement. It's time to show right-wing special interests just how much grassroots power we hold.** [**To do it, Jay needs you to be one of our 2,000 grassroots donors today. We can't rest on our laurels -- we just have too much at stake.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ac19/2f5ea88d/7a8578c7/2cbda0e2/934609615/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-7_freeze-2&refcode=ema_2018-12-7_freeze-2&utm_campaign=freeze-2&utm_medium=ema") Thanks for helping us out, Aisling Kerins | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ac19/2f5ea88d/7a8578c7/2cbda0e2/934609615/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-7_freeze-2&refcode=ema_2018-12-7_freeze-2&utm_campaign=freeze-2&utm_medium=ema")
---

_Now more than ever, we must support progressive leadership in America. Chip in to show you're with Jay Inslee as we fight for progress in Washington state and around the country._

| [CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7ac19/2f5ea88d/7a8578c7/2cbda0e1/934609615/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-contribute-button")

name: Press Secretary Garriga
subject: Join Rep. Anderson Dec. 20th
body:
Representative Jeramey Anderson of Mississippi's 110th House District will host his fifth State of the District Address on December 20, 2018

---
| | | | 2018 State of the District
---
| |
---
| | Representative Jeramey Anderson of Mississippi's 110th House District will host his fifth State of the District Address on December 20, 2018 at 6:00pm. The State of the District will be held at Magnolia Junior High School in the City of Moss Point.
---
| |
---
| | "Representative Anderson's address will primarily focus on key issues that affect the district; including but not limited to voting rights, education, healthcare, infrastructure and creating opportunities for upward mobility. He will also include briefs regarding the outcomes from the previous session and the summer special session." said Press Secretary, Melissa Garriga. She added, "they will also be exploring legislation aimed at creating positive changes for the state as the new session approaches in January." The Representative's Office is committed to ensuring that there are plenty of ways for constituents to take part in the State of the District, including a new way to watch the speech and share exclusive graphics with your social networks, and opportunities to discuss Representative Anderson's remarks immediately following the address.
---
| | | [**GET YOUR FREE TICKETS**](https://u6371721.ct.sendgrid.net/wf/click?upn=JILbql1UFnMuMynxZZdqsyqxnzueLsvnS5k-2BIqMwJG6F6GcJGpgi9UiGHa5eG4qpTiSv6EzAiRDdErXikOXcYTkmqtHzbCmqfxHlGG7JN9heE7a0WcBy89CnIYEwHd6Z3r01Pz0EHwCdQuwqKlJNZC-2Fpm-2BQKGTfScNR6VXGzNTywAPweotz57QcnYBjQtBZKGs0EOqrzyKMjhVjKBbQExWI0JGm7je12nlkzGn2zHB0a4C3LGsdw3Y-2Fe1SGFsineuklGqV-2FWJEvwfnxs8O0azJgPYZCqGvOcJCR7NH7dGsCB0oLcH-2FQPpkhEGZIcy-2BLG_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFTZG3zAd2sHA7NWAFdIVBISw2lyEWyZSZJRiFshCpvAnr7fUUJ2fp7WqKJepDVzxLCdb2iThLP2Yn8TCvAe0SvrhODeaZm78QsM-2FqIMb1VHZxObMTkUJbZmzUqHe-2FFlzcoHGf23AufJeLTQrzbTMaOd1kjXz0T9-2FMfVUYVUuq4jzKYYcj05-2FDzFOQEtlf-2BkyiO1CVHn42PFBIELG6eeWl5dUIN1fmU7XSyM5BwH322Ixp9AOU-2Bo4WLYZkBOQFtYauLdXumY9dA3QXmVFwfZ08Optl6YdkGEBI2NIAK5-2F2bESoWqe6l8ShVwBpj4E3fwA2hHaOObEbGIFqjS1ePkUonKLIgRAw3q-2BfHtGczXKqhSiSJADXYsTcDGObOxAvcuelZ1MbbDJy719tecKESKdnzdf2vsFKeIPL9eUF4rstsL1wjB9vqzQzNlzDANbzF341NTKjrXZgPq8Il8yVvuh7ZZWVc7WEdKQ7qUomCjVs7d4uryq8ELfY1Q2k0iuS1gFMsJK3SrL7Mr3q1dgY-2FsoHHsEtR-2BF2BdbiH6HfKZxIKfw-3D-3D)
---
| | If you cannot attend you can still watch the State of the District from your home or on your smartphone; we will be streaming a version of the speech.
---

clean_body: Representative Jeramey Anderson of Mississippi's 110th House District will host his fifth State of the District Address on December 20, 2018 | | | | | | 2018 State of the District | | | | Representative Jeramey Anderson of Mississippi's 110th House District will host his fifth State of the District Address on December 20, 2018 at 6:00pm. The State of the District will be held at Magnolia Junior High School in the City of Moss Point. | | | | "Representative Anderson's address will primarily focus on key issues that affect the district; including but not limited to voting rights, education, healthcare, infrastructure and creating opportunities for upward mobility. He will also include briefs regarding the outcomes from the previous session and the summer special session." said Press Secretary, Melissa Garriga. She added, "they will also be exploring legislation aimed at creating positive changes for the state as the new session approaches in January." The Representative's Office is committed to ensuring that there are plenty of ways for constituents to take part in the State of the District, including a new way to watch the speech and share exclusive graphics with your social networks, and opportunities to discuss Representative Anderson's remarks immediately following the address. | | | | | If you cannot attend you can still watch the State of the District from your home or on your smartphone; we will be streaming a version of the speech. | | | | | You can also tune in on Facebook and YouTube. In the meantime, you can learn more about how you can watch, share and discuss the speech. You can sign up to get exclusive content before and after the speech by visiting . Make sure you're following on Twitter for news, real-time updates before, during, and after the address. The official hashtag for this year's State of the District is #SOTD110 on all social media platforms. If you have any questions, please contact press secretary, Melissa Garriga at 228-206-2129 Ext.3 or . | Share Via: | | |

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Loading...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | No one wants to log onto their favorite website and see this: | [](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffdbf/4462dc26/89d0622f/201efa18/342663022/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181208m")
---
But if we don't work to restore net neutrality protections, the buffering wheel of annoyance could become a whole lot more common.
The FCC decided to repeal Obama-era net neutrality protections. Without those protections, Internet Service Providers can throttle websites and charge for "fast lanes" to deliver specific content faster.
**The Senate passed legislation to overturn the FCC repeal. But if the House doesn't do the same before December 10th, the FCC's repeal stays in effect.**
[**Demand the House act before Monday's deadline -- sign my petition and then make sure to share it on Facebook and Twitter. The more noise we make on this, the better.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffdbf/4462dc26/89d0622f/201efa18/342663022/VEsE/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181208m")
With gratitude,
Sherrod

|
